one_time_description: Williams Treaty settlement payments: $98,676,000 from ISC and $65,784,000 from Province of Ontario recognized as revenue in 2019; significant distribution to members of $2,781,374 in 2020 and $109,666,380 in 2019 related to Williams Treaty
economic_notes: Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership gaming revenue program generates funds. Economic development includes resource centre, cottage and small business centre rentals. Subdivision development (lot 12/13) with $2,969,262 in commitments. Williams Treaty settlement funds held in restricted cash of $60,108,433. Trust fund (Islands in the Trent Youth Allocation Fund) of $3,905,081 not consolidated.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Prior year (2019) revenues and expenses were extraordinarily large due to Williams Treaty settlement: $98,676,000 from ISC and $65,784,000 from Province of Ontario, plus related member distributions of ~$109.7M. Williams Treaty restricted cash of $60,108,433 held internally restricted. No related entities owned or controlled by First Nation per note 1. Trust Fund (Islands in the Trent Youth Allocation Fund, $3,905,081) reported separately and not consolidated. COVID-19 measurement uncertainty disclosed. No schedule of remuneration provided. Accounts receivable includes Due from Government $1,167,884 and Other accounts receivable $326,117. Current liabilities estimated from accounts payable $6,890,446 plus due to government $696,446 less long-term portions. Land management expenses include $2,781,374 Williams Treaty distribution to members in 2020.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
